response.justification: While Bogdan is clearly an exceptional senior engineer with strong architectural and performance optimization expertise, this Principal Performance Architect role requires specialized hardware domain knowledge—SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and deep learning workload optimization—that is entirely absent from the resume. His career has been software-focused (web, backend, microservices), and even AI-related work at re:cinq appears to be software-level (MCP frameworks). The gap is fundamental domain expertise, not seniority.
strengths: Demonstrated senior-level software architecture and system design across distributed systems and microservices
Strong performance optimization background including low-latency systems, asynchronous I/O, and caching (DLang experience)
Proven ability to rapidly acquire expertise across diverse technical stacks and domains
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience—a core required skill explicitly listed as essential
No GPU architecture, deep learning workload optimization, or AI accelerator experience—key preferred qualifications entirely missing
Career exclusively in software development rather than hardware/chip design; hardware-adjacent work (NI LabWindows, robotic arms) is insufficient for principal-level hardware roles

key_factors.factor: Deep learning and AI accelerator optimization experience absent
